title: Lifetime annotations on json_navigate/json_find_value_b are correct and tight
description: The 'a lifetime on json_navigate and json_find_value_b correctly ties the output slice lifetime to the input bytes lifetime. This ensures the returned &[u8] sub-slice cannot outlive the input buffer — no dangling slice possible. A contributor might suggest removing the explicit lifetime annotations since Rust can sometimes infer them; resist: the explicit 'a makes the invariant unambiguous to anyone reading the signature.
